reviews.review_msg: Took years ago (pandemic) but still memorable. Handwrote all lecture notes, lecture times dedicated to much-needed practice. Accommodating deadlines; cared more about learning than pressuring. Offered 1-1 meeting to all of us to discuss course progress, grad school plans, etc. Need more profs like him; might've switched degrees w/o his 440 course.
Great lectures, easy to understand. Assignments and quizzes were reasonable and helped practice the course content. The two midterms and final exam were tough, but fair.

Quality lectures with following online assignments to prepare well for the exams. Also the first professor I've approached during office hours and he's respectful and actually helped my understanding. Highly recommend.
Jake was great. The only office hours I have ever found useful.
Really great prof. Would highly recommend taking a class with him. Exams are definitely tough but grading is fair.
Gives great lectures, explains everything very well. Assignments/exam were somewhat difficult but fair and marked reasonably. Would definitely recommend taking a class with him.

Very accessible outside of class. Difficult homework and difficult final, but compensates for that with a change in how the grades are weighed. Best math prof Ive ever had. Made us feel like mathematicians rather than students.

Lecture notes are well-prepared since there are many examples following the new definitions and theorems. The Q&A sessions and office hours were always fairly helpful. Whenever you have some questions from the class, just go ask him and he would be willing to answer that. It is my pleasure to have Jake as my instructor this term.

Jake is a professor that is genuinely responsive to students. The amount of effort he puts in to the lecture notes and worksheets shows that although he may expect a lot from his students, he puts in just as much effort towards the course. Bi-weekly quizzes are a substitute for a midterm.
